purpose: Use legally available literature metadata and user-provided files to iteratively improve transferable MOE pharmacophore mechanism curation rules.
important_boundary: This corpus stores bibliographic and methodological metadata. It must not store proprietary MOE files, license data, paywalled full text, or target-specific rules inside generic curation rules.

method_classes.ligand_based_training_set: Pharmacophore generated from active/inactive ligand training sets, commonly HypoGen/common feature/SAR driven.
method_classes.structure_based_moe_query: Pharmacophore generated from a structure, binding pocket, ligand or peptide complex, commonly via MOE Query Editor or receptor-ligand interaction features.
method_classes.hybrid_virtual_screening: Pharmacophore used together with docking, MD, MM/PBSA or experimental validation.

entries.title: Pharmacophore Modeling and Virtual Screening Studies to Identify New cAMP Phosphodiesterase 4 Inhibitors
entries.reported_pharmacophore: HypoGen ligand-based model; not a pure MOE structure-derived query.
entries.reconstruction_level_possible: L1 feature_family if feature list is extracted; L3 requires original training set and HypoGen settings.
entries.value_for_v2: Separates ligand-training-set pharmacophore logic from structure-contact MOE logic; reinforces that structure curation cannot be claimed as exact HypoGen reproduction.

entries.title: Establishment of screening protocol for identification of aminopeptidase N inhibitors
entries.reported_pharmacophore: Ligand-based or hybrid screening protocol; article-specific settings must be extracted before structure reproduction.
entries.reconstruction_level_possible: L0-L1 from abstract/metadata; higher levels require full method and supporting data.
entries.value_for_v2: Tests whether the workflow can declare insufficient public input instead of overclaiming strict reproduction.

entries.title: The discovery of potential tubulin inhibitors: a combination of pharmacophore modeling, virtual screening and molecular docking studies
entries.reconstruction_level_possible: Depends on availability of training set, feature definitions, and docking receptor state.
entries.value_for_v2: Supports separating pharmacophore filtering from docking ranking during failure attribution.

entries.title: PARP-based pharmacophore model development, virtual screening, and molecular docking studies
entries.reported_pharmacophore: Pharmacophore plus docking workflow, likely ligand-based or mixed protocol.
entries.reconstruction_level_possible: L0-L1 unless full feature geometry and training set are available.
entries.value_for_v2: Tests PARP/nucleotide-like pocket handling and ligand-based versus structure-based boundary.

entries.title: Structure-based pharmacophore modeling, virtual screening, molecular docking and biological evaluation for discovering novel PLK1-PBD inhibitors

reported_public_inputs.structure: PDB complex publicly referenced in article
reported_public_inputs.model: Five-feature model selected by validation statistics; reported families include hydrophobic, anionic/acceptor, acceptor, donor/acceptor, and aromatic roles.

entries.reconstruction_level_possible: L2-L3 if article figure/feature table is extracted and MOE Query Editor settings are reproduced.
entries.value_for_v2: High-value article for strict MOE Query Editor reconstruction and mechanism compression of phosphorylated peptide-binding pockets.

entries.title: Structure-Based Pharmacophore Modeling, Virtual Screening, Molecular Docking and Biological Evaluation for Identification of Potential PARP-1 Inhibitors

reported_public_inputs.structure: Article reports protein-ligand complex structures and a final pharmacophore model.
reported_public_inputs.validation: Pharmacophore model selection uses validation against active/inactive or decoy-like datasets.

entries.reconstruction_level_possible: L2-L3 if final feature geometry and selected PDB/ligand state are extracted.
entries.value_for_v2: Improves rules for ligand-induced pocket pharmacophores, cofactor-like pocket constraints, and GH/enrichment-aware model selection.

entries.title: Structure-Based Pharmacophore Design and Virtual Screening for Novel Tubulin Inhibitors
entries.reported_pharmacophore: Structure-based pharmacophore and virtual screening workflow.
entries.reconstruction_level_possible: L1-L3 depending on public PDB/ligand/feature figure extraction.
entries.value_for_v2: Improves deep hydrophobic pocket, aromatic wall, and ligand-shape boundary rules.

entries.title: Discovery of a Dual Tubulin and Poly(ADP-Ribose) Polymerase-1 Inhibitor by Structure-Based Pharmacophore Modeling, Virtual Screening, Molecular Docking, Molecular Dynamics Simulation, and Biological Evaluation
entries.local_user_file_hint: ja1c12075_si_001.pdf may contain supporting information in the user's literature folder.
entries.reported_pharmacophore: Dual-target, structure-based pharmacophore plus docking/MD/biological evaluation workflow.
entries.reconstruction_level_possible: L2-L3 for each target if structures and feature models are fully extracted; full pipeline requires docking and MD settings.
entries.value_for_v2: Improves multi-target pharmacophore comparison, cross-target feature pruning, and evidence-layer separation.

entries.title: Dual S-RBD/NRP1-targeting peptide discovery using pharmacophore and structure-based virtual screening
entries.source_url: user-provided local PDF or publisher page required for exact metadata
entries.local_user_file_hint: Discovery of dual S-RBD NRP1-targeting peptides*.pdf in local literature folders
entries.reported_pharmacophore: MOE pharmacophore query or structure-derived peptide screening model.
entries.reconstruction_level_possible: L2-L3 when article feature list and PDB chains are extracted.
entries.value_for_v2: Improves receptor-side peptide interface models and distinction between receptor-side and ligand-side feature coordinates.

entries.title: Highly potent, selective, biostable, and cell-permeable cyclic D-peptide for dual-targeting therapy of lung cancer
entries.reported_pharmacophore: NRP1-BD pharmacophore features reported in text; dual-target cyclic peptide design and validation.
entries.reconstruction_level_possible: L2-L3 if feature list and receptor/peptide state are extracted.
entries.value_for_v2: Improves cyclic peptide, CendR-like motif, terminal clamp, and receptor-side versus peptide-side feature handling.
